The sheen was traced to oily water overflowing from a drip pan beneath the export pipeline shutdown valve (SDV). The drip pan, which contained residual oil, was suspected to have filled up due to recent heavy downpours. Accumulated rainwater displaced the residual oil, leading to intermittent drips to sea. No hydrocarbon leak was observed directly from the SDV. Platform was previously shut-in and secured.

The Agency got a report of the incident on the 14th September, 2024 and called the operator to verify the alleged oil spill on Nembe Well 13 Wellhead. The Company later mobilized for JIV on the 19th of September, 2024. Addressing the JIT the company representative explained that the incident was at Well 35 and not Well 13 as it was previously reported by the representatives of Nembe Community. i. The incident point at the Well 35 Wellhead showed evidence of sabotage as the maintenance team listed the parts of the well head that was vandalized and taken away as follows; a. 2 pieces 2’’/9’’/16’’ gate/seat/bonnet/blind b. 2 pieces of 2’’/1’’/16’’ gate/seat/bonnet/blind flanges on s/s outlet c. 4 pieces of 2’’ blind flanges/bull plugs with ½’’ KTV on annuls A/B of both arms. d. 18 pieces of 7/8 studs and nuts. e. 6 pieces of RX24 ring gaskets. f. 1 piece of KTV on tree cap. ii. As at the time of JIV, there were emission of gases on the Well 35 wellhead this limited the JIT from getting too close to the wellhead. iii. There were visible sheen and light crude on the surface of the water body. iv. The JIT observed that there were no security/surveillance camp within and around the wellhead.

Site location name: 18” Kolocreek -Gbaran CPF @ Fandud Onopa Community in Yenagoa LGA, Bayelsa State.
Description of impact: i. There was a pool of water with light crude on its surface at the incident point. ii. Upon bailing of the water and manual excavation, a 3” Valve installed at 12 O’ Clock position on the pipeline was seen with a 2” galvanized pipe connected to the valve through an elbow. The 2” galvanized pipe extended to an unknown destination though suspected to be the illegal bunkering camp where the explosion occurred iii. While the excavation was on going, the JIT noticed smoke in the suspected illegal bunkering site which prompted the JIT to conduct the JIV without properly exposing the entire circumference of the pipe. iv. Due to security concerns, the JIT was disallowed from extending the investigation to the illegal bunkering site where the explosion occurred. v. Free phase crude oil was seen within and outside the right of way. The JIT followed the plume of crude oil to some areas with burnt vegetation. It was suspected that the fire emanated from the camp and extended toward the pipeline right of way, though the pipeline right of way was not affected by the fire.
